0.original: Mr. Activationist plans a vacation. 2 00:00:04,975 --> 00:00:05,984 He takes it. 3 00:00:06,255 --> 00:00:12,144 Mr. Passivationist plans a vacation, but he postpones it until next year. 4 00:00:12,415 --> 00:00:16,065 Mr. A decides he should attend church regularly. 5 00:00:16,255 --> 00:00:17,105 He does. 6 00:00:17,294 --> 00:00:21,105 Mr. P thinks it's a good idea to go to church regularly, 7 00:00:21,135 --> 00:00:25,665 too, but he finds ways to postpone acquiring this new habit. 8 00:00:26,174 --> 00:00:32,305 Mr. A feels that he should drop a note to someone he knows to congratulate him on some achievement. 9 00:00:32,334 --> 00:00:33,665 He writes the note. 10 00:00:34,014 --> 00:00:39,745 Under the same circumstances, Mr. P finds a good reason to put off writing the note, 11 00:00:39,774 --> 00:00:41,505 and it never gets written. 12 00:00:42,415 --> 00:00:45,105 The difference shows up in big things, too. 13 00:00:45,373 --> 00:00:48,225 Mr. A wants to go into business for himself. 14 00:00:48,255 --> 00:00:49,185 He does. 15 00:00:49,535 --> 00:00:58,385 Mr. P also wants to go into business for himself, but he discovers just in the nick of time a good reason why he had better not. 16 00:00:58,734 --> 00:01:03,185 Mr. A, age forty, decides he wants to take up a new line of work. 17 00:01:03,294 --> 00:01:04,225 He does. 18 00:01:04,494 --> 00:01:10,704 The same idea occurs to Mr. P, but he debates himself out of doing anything about it. 19 00:01:11,374 --> 00:01:18,465 The difference between Messer's activationist and passivationist shows through in all forms of behavior. 20 00:01:18,894 --> 00:01:25,105 Mr. A gets the things done he wants done, and as byproducts he gains confidence, 21 00:01:25,135 --> 00:01:29,505 a feeling of inner security, self-reliance, and more income. 22 00:01:30,174 --> 00:01:34,945 Mr. P doesn't get the things done he wants done because he won't act. 23 00:01:35,054 --> 00:01:42,225 As byproducts, he loses confidence in himself, destroys his self-reliance, lives in mediocrity. 24 00:01:42,894 --> 00:01:45,423 Mr. Activationist does. 25 00:01:45,614 --> 00:01:49,744 Mr. Passivationist is going to do, but doesn't. 26 00:01:50,415 --> 00:01:52,864 Everyone wants to be an Activationist. 27 00:01:52,975 --> 00:01:55,504 So, let's get the action habit. 28 00:01:56,174 --> 00:02:06,064 A lot of Passivationists got that way because they insisted on waiting until everything was 100% favorable before they took action. 29 00:02:06,415 --> 00:02:15,025 Perfection is highly desirable, but nothing man-made or man-designed is or can be absolutely perfect. 30 00:02:15,215 --> 00:02:19,744 So to wait for the perfect set of conditions is to wait forever. 31 00:02:21,134 --> 00:02:29,105 Here are three case histories that show how three persons reacted to conditions.
0.b1: Mr. Activationist plans a vacation and takes it. Mr. Passivationist plans a vacation but delays it until next year. Mr. A decides to attend church regularly and does so. Mr. P also thinks going to church regularly is a good idea, but he finds ways to delay starting this new habit. Mr. A feels he should write a congratulatory note to someone he knows, and he writes it. In the same situation, Mr. P finds a good reason to put off writing the note, and it is never written. The difference is also seen in bigger matters. Mr. A wants to start his own business, and he does. Mr. P also wants to start his own business, but at the last moment, he finds a good reason why it's better not to. Mr. A, at forty, decides he wants to change his job, and he does. Mr. P has the same idea, but he talks himself out of doing anything about it. The difference between Mr. Messer's activationist and passivationist shows in all behaviors. Mr. A gets done what he wants done, and as a result, he gains confidence, inner security, self-reliance, and more money. Mr. P doesn't get done what he wants done because he doesn't act. As a result, he loses confidence, his self-reliance is damaged, and he lives an ordinary life. Mr. Activationist acts. Mr. Passivationist intends to act but doesn't. Everyone wants to be an Activationist. So, let's develop the habit of taking action. Many Passivationists became that way because they waited for everything to be 100% perfect before acting. Perfection is very good, but nothing made by humans can be absolutely perfect. So, waiting for perfect conditions means waiting forever. Here are three stories showing how three people responded to situations.
0.cn: 激活主义者先生计划休假并去休假了。消极主义者先生计划休假，但推迟到明年。A先生决定定期去教堂，并且这样做了。P先生也认为定期去教堂是个好主意，但他找各种理由推迟养成这个新习惯。A先生觉得他应该给认识的人写信祝贺他取得成就，他写了这封信。在同样的情况下，P先生找到了一个好理由推迟写这封信，结果这封信从未被写成。这种区别在大事情上也很明显。A先生想自己创业，他做到了。P先生也想自己创业，但在最后一刻，他找到了一个充分的理由说明他最好不要这样做。四十岁的A先生决定想换一份新工作，他做到了。P先生也有同样的想法，但他劝说自己不要采取任何行动。梅瑟先生的激活主义者和消极主义者之间的区别体现在所有行为中。A先生完成了他想做的事情，作为回报，他获得了自信、内心的安全感、自力更生和更多的收入。P先生没有完成他想做的事情，因为他不行动。作为结果，他失去了自信，破坏了自力更生，生活在平庸之中。激活主义者先生做到了。消极主义者先生打算做，但没有做。每个人都想成为一个激活主义者。所以，让我们养成行动的习惯。许多消极主义者之所以变成这样，是因为他们坚持等到一切都百分之百有利时才采取行动。完美是高度可取的，但人类制造或设计的东西不可能绝对完美。所以，等待完美的条件就是永远等待。这里有三个案例，展示了三个人如何应对情况。
0.ai_notes:
'Activationist': a person who takes action to achieve goals.
'Passivationist': a person who tends to delay or avoid taking action.
'postpones': to delay something until a later time.
'drop a note': a casual way to say writing a short letter or message to someone.
'put off': a phrasal verb meaning to delay or postpone.
'go into business for himself': to start one's own company or work independently.
'in the nick of time': an idiom meaning at the very last possible moment.
'take up a new line of work': to start a new career or job.
'debates himself out of': to talk oneself out of something; to convince oneself not to do something.
'byproducts': secondary results or consequences that happen while doing something else.
'self-reliance': the ability to depend on one's own powers and resources.
'mediocrity': the state of being average or not very good; a life without significant achievement.
'action habit': the practice of consistently taking steps toward a goal without unnecessary delay.
'100% favorable': a situation where everything is perfect and there are no risks.
0.remarks:
'Activationist': 为实现目标而采取行动的人。
'Passivationist': 倾向于拖延或避免采取行动的人。
'postpones': 将某事推迟到以后。
'drop a note': 非正式地写封短信或留言。
'put off': 推迟或延期。
'go into business for himself': 创办自己的公司或独立工作。
'in the nick of time': 在最后一刻。
'take up a new line of work': 开始新的职业或工作。
'debates himself out of': 说服自己不要做某事。
'byproducts': 在做某事过程中产生的次要结果或后果。
'self-reliance': 依靠自身力量和资源的能力。
'mediocrity': 平庸的状态；没有显著成就的生活。
'action habit': 为实现目标持续采取行动而不无故拖延的习惯。
'100% favorable': 一切完美且没有风险的情况。
